rationale: Educ. Code §45.105 governs how public school funds may be spent, not how property tax exemptions are structured. Increasing the homestead exemption indirectly reduces local district tax revenue but does not conflict with any expenditure authorization in this section.
quote: Local school funds from district taxes, tuition fees of students not entitled to a free education, other local sources, and state funds not designated for a specific purpose may be used for the purposes listed for state and county available funds
_note: No direct conflict. §45.105 addresses authorized expenditures, not tax policy or exemption amounts. Fiscal impact on local school funds is indirect and not prohibited by this section.
